document.diagnosis: Iteration 13 was dominated by candidate-generation failure: five-answer banks contained a correct answer on only 2/12 cases, nine-answer banks on 4/12, and every base plurality scored 0/12. The retained five-bank three-review champion recovered three answers, achieved 25% in every family, and had the best partial accuracy (69.7%) with no harmful reversal. Review remains useful, but the nine-bank reviewers converted greater oracle coverage into only 2/12 exact, showing that larger banks also increase selection burden. The next batch should therefore retain the balanced champion, combine broader generation with the lower-variance one-review mechanism, replicate the efficient five-bank reviewer, and test committee compression as the orthogonal organization.
extensions_requested: Add a shared_evidence_board base-generation primitive: three independent scouts post shuffled concise claims, evidence, and counterchecks; subsequent base answerers independently derive exact candidates while explicitly testing the board. Count every call and expose neither family labels nor the answer key.
most_important_evidence: The five-bank candidate oracle was only 2/12 and the nine-bank oracle only 4/12; all observed base pluralities were 0/12.
Five-bank three-review repair reached 3/12 with one correct result in each family, three helpful interventions, zero harmful interventions, and 69.7% partial accuracy.
Nine-bank three-review repair had twice the five-bank oracle coverage but finished at 2/12, with no constraint or logic successes; broader generation alone did not solve candidate selection.

Five-bank one-review repair is now 20/66 exact across its five observed panels, with 15 helpful versus two harmful interventions. In Iteration 13 it used 5.92 mean calls versus 7.75 for three-review repair.
Every strategy other than the retained current winner had 0% weakest-family accuracy in Iteration 13, so its balanced 25% result should not be displaced based on pooled accuracy alone.

proposals.hypothesis: Iteration 13's nine-answer bank doubled candidate-oracle coverage from 2/12 to 4/12, while the one-review mechanism has a pooled 15-to-2 helpful-to-harmful intervention balance. Pairing them tests whether one falsifying repairer can exploit broader generation without three-review fragmentation.
proposals.name: One-Review Nine-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Replicate the unchanged call-efficient mechanism because its strongly positive pooled correction balance remains promising despite noisy exact accuracy, and it requires two fewer review calls than the retained champion whenever review activates.
proposals.name: One-Review Five-Bank Falsifying Repair

proposals.hypothesis: Three disjoint committee delegates may compress a diverse nine-answer bank into a smaller, less distracting evidence set. Routing one answer-generating falsifier only on committee disagreement tests this orthogonal aggregation architecture without recreating a large repair panel.
proposals.name: Committee Delegates with One Falsifying Repairer

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the unchanged nine-bank three-review challenger for replication: it is 37/96 exact through Iteration 13 and remains the established test of whether higher candidate coverage can outweigh additional distractors.
proposals.name: Nine Solvers plus Three Falsifying Repairers

proposals.hypothesis: A fifteen-answer deterministic plurality tests whether independent scale alone improves exact aggregation on panels where five-answer plurality fails, without introducing reviewer variance or another decision rule.
proposals.name: Fifteen-Vote Plurality

research_hypotheses: The immediate bottleneck is producing at least one correct candidate, especially for constraint and logic cases, rather than choosing among already-correct majorities.
A nine-bank, one-review system can combine higher candidate coverage with the favorable correction balance of single-review repair.
Committee delegates will help if repeated partial reasoning is more reliable than isolated raw candidates; they will hurt if correct answers usually occur as unique minorities inside committees.
A fifteen-answer plurality will distinguish benefits from independent sampling scale from benefits attributable to repair.
A shared evidence board may improve absent-answer creation by letting final answerers reuse independently discovered decisive evidence without family-specific routing.
document.risk_note: All estimates remain noisy on 12-case panels. Committee compression can discard a correct raw minority, and a larger bank can add distractors without improving plurality. With the three automatic retentions, any three of these proposals remain within budget: the worst selectable combination costs at most 288 Luna calls (180 shared base calls plus 108 review calls). No development result should be described as general until a frozen mechanism transfers to untouched exact-verifiable families.
